title: ABNF, Tokens, Whitespace, Line Endings, Limits, and Parser Architecture · Contract Triage
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but defective strict bounded incremental parser architecture; retain the raw transcript, isolate the first divergent state, and preserve a regression corpus entry. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in strict bounded incremental parser architecture and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Translate RFC ABNF alternatives, repetition, terminals, lists, and prose constraints into explicit parser states and tests.
Validate tokens and visible bytes using octet classes while rejecting separators, controls, NUL, and forbidden whitespace.
Require CRLF at HTTP/1.1 structural boundaries and document any local robustness exception without parser disagreement.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Order the regression proof · Debug Parse Validate Effect Phases
steps.instruction:
Order the Parse Validate Effect Phases evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Order the regression proof · Debug Parse Validate Effect Phases.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Parse Validate Effect Phases assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Order the regression proof · Debug Parse Validate Effect Phases, keep Parse Validate Effect Phases connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.text: A debug protocol team handles case 8649 in a canary edge service advertising HTTP/3 with bounded fallback. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate strict grammar and resource limits before any application effect; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then append a second pipelined message and prove the first parser leaves the exact residual bytes. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate byte recognition, structural validation, semantic validation, policy, and effects so failure cannot partially authorize work.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Parse Validate Effect Phases has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.
